name: menubar
description: Horizontal application menu bar with keyboard-navigable dropdown menus

meta.name: Menubar
meta.description: Horizontal application menu bar with keyboard-navigable dropdown menus

props.name: aria-label
props.description: Accessible label used when no visible label is present.

props.name: className
props.description: Additional CSS class names merged onto the root element.

examples.title: Basic
examples.code: <Menubar aria-label="Main" menus={[{ id: "file", label: "File", items: [{ id: "new", label: "New", onSelect: () => {} }] }]} />

whenToUse: A persistent application-style command bar (File / Edit / View) where each top-level entry opens a menu of actions
Desktop-like apps that expose grouped commands across a horizontal bar
whenNotToUse: Site or section navigation between pages or destinations — use NavigationMenu
A single trigger that opens one menu of actions — use a Menu/Dropdown
Switching between peer content panels — use Tabs

antiPatterns.bad: Using Menubar entries as page links
antiPatterns.why: menubar implies commands/actions within the current view, not navigation to other pages

related.name: NavigationMenu
related.reason: NavigationMenu navigates between destinations; Menubar invokes commands

related.name: Menu
related.reason: Each Menubar entry behaves like a single Menu anchored under its trigger

intent.a11yRationale: Implements the WAI-ARIA menubar pattern: a roving-tabindex row of menuitem triggers (Left/Right/Home/End) where ArrowDown opens the menu and focuses its first item, Up/Down move within the menu, and Escape closes and restores focus to the trigger

flexibility.note: Menus are described by data (menus prop) so roles and keyboard wiring stay correct
Surface, borders, and focus ring must resolve to --cascivo-* tokens
